feat(telemetry): Add CPU metrics collection from /proc/stat

Implement CPU utilization tracking for worker telemetry:
- CpuStats: Raw stats from /proc/stat with parsing
- LoadAverage: 1/5/15 min load averages from /proc/loadavg
- CpuPressureStall: PSI data from /proc/pressure/cpu (Linux 4.20+)
- CpuTelemetry: Aggregated telemetry snapshot with percentage calculation
- Per-core CPU statistics collection
- Counter overflow protection using saturating arithmetic
- Comprehensive test suite with 19 unit tests

Part of bead dmg: Worker Telemetry Collection Agent (CPU Metrics)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement a pure-Rust disk I/O benchmark measuring read/write throughput and latency patterns relevant to compilation workloads.

## Background and Justification
Disk I/O affects compilation through:
- Reading source files and dependencies
- Writing incremental compilation caches
- Building and linking final binaries

Workers with slow disks bottleneck the entire build pipeline.

## Implementation Details

### Benchmark Design
1. **Sequential write throughput**: Simulates writing compilation artifacts
2. **Sequential read throughput**: Simulates reading source files
3. **Random read latency**: Simulates incremental cache lookups
4. **fsync latency**: Measures durability overhead

### Disk Benchmark Implementation
```rust
use std::fs::{self, File, OpenOptions};
use std::io::{Read, Write, Seek, SeekFrom};
use std::time::Instant;
use tempfile::TempDir;

pub struct DiskBenchmarkResult {
    pub score: f64,
    pub seq_write_mbps: f64,
    pub seq_read_mbps: f64,
    pub random_read_iops: f64,
    pub fsync_latency_ms: f64,
}

/// Sequential write throughput
fn sequential_write_benchmark(dir: &Path) -> f64 {
    let path = dir.join("seq_write_test");
    let mut file = File::create(&path).unwrap();
    
    const BLOCK_SIZE: usize = 64 * 1024;  // 64KB blocks
    const TOTAL_SIZE: usize = 256 * 1024 * 1024;  // 256MB total
    let block = vec![0xAAu8; BLOCK_SIZE];
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut written = 0;
    while written < TOTAL_SIZE {
        file.write_all(&block).unwrap();
        written += BLOCK_SIZE;
    }
    file.sync_all().unwrap();
    let duration = start.elapsed();
    
    fs::remove_file(&path).ok();
    
    (TOTAL_SIZE as f64 / 1024.0 / 1024.0) / duration.as_secs_f64()
}

/// Sequential read throughput
fn sequential_read_benchmark(dir: &Path) -> f64 {
    // First create test file
    let path = dir.join("seq_read_test");
    let test_data = vec![0xBBu8; 256 * 1024 * 1024];
    fs::write(&path, &test_data).unwrap();
    
    // Clear page cache if possible (Linux)
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    {
        let _ = std::process::Command::new("sync").status();
        let _ = fs::write("/proc/sys/vm/drop_caches", "1");
    }
    
    let mut file = File::open(&path).unwrap();
    let mut buffer = vec![0u8; 64 * 1024];
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut total_read = 0;
    loop {
        match file.read(&mut buffer) {
            Ok(0) => break,
            Ok(n) => total_read += n,
            Err(_) => break,
        }
    }
    let duration = start.elapsed();
    
    fs::remove_file(&path).ok();
    
    (total_read as f64 / 1024.0 / 1024.0) / duration.as_secs_f64()
}

/// Random read IOPS
fn random_read_benchmark(dir: &Path) -> f64 {
    let path = dir.join("random_read_test");
    let test_data = vec![0xCCu8; 64 * 1024 * 1024];  // 64MB file
    fs::write(&path, &test_data).unwrap();
    
    let mut file = File::open(&path).unwrap();
    let file_size = 64 * 1024 * 1024u64;
    let mut buffer = [0u8; 4096];  // 4KB reads
    
    // Generate deterministic random offsets
    let mut rng_state = 54321u64;
    let offsets: Vec<u64> = (0..10000).map(|_| {
        rng_state = rng_state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1);
        (rng_state % (file_size - 4096)) & !4095  // Align to 4KB
    }).collect();
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    for &offset in &offsets {
        file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(offset)).unwrap();
        file.read_exact(&mut buffer).unwrap();
    }
    let duration = start.elapsed();
    
    fs::remove_file(&path).ok();
    
    offsets.len() as f64 / duration.as_secs_f64()
}

/// fsync latency
fn fsync_benchmark(dir: &Path) -> f64 {
    let path = dir.join("fsync_test");
    let mut file = OpenOptions::new()
        .create(true)
        .write(true)
        .open(&path)
        .unwrap();
    
    let data = vec![0xDDu8; 4096];
    let iterations = 100;
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    for _ in 0..iterations {
        file.write_all(&data).unwrap();
        file.sync_all().unwrap();
    }
    let duration = start.elapsed();
    
    fs::remove_file(&path).ok();
    
    duration.as_millis() as f64 / iterations as f64
}

pub fn run_disk_benchmark() -> DiskBenchmarkResult {
    let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let dir = temp_dir.path();
    
    let seq_write = sequential_write_benchmark(dir);
    let seq_read = sequential_read_benchmark(dir);
    let random_iops = random_read_benchmark(dir);
    let fsync_latency = fsync_benchmark(dir);
    
    // Weighted score (read throughput most important)
    let score = (seq_read * 2.0) + seq_write + (random_iops / 1000.0) * 50.0 + 
                (100.0 / fsync_latency) * 10.0;
    
    DiskBenchmarkResult {
        score,
        seq_write_mbps: seq_write,
        seq_read_mbps: seq_read,
        random_read_iops: random_iops,
        fsync_latency_ms: fsync_latency,
    }
}
```

#[test]
fn test_random_read_iops() {
    info!("TEST START: test_random_read_iops");
    let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    info!("INPUT: random_read_benchmark() with 10k random 4KB reads");
    let iops = random_read_benchmark(temp_dir.path());
    info!("RESULT: Random read IOPS = {}", iops);
    assert!(iops > 100.0);  // At least 100 IOPS
    info!("VERIFY: Random IOPS {} exceeds minimum 100", iops);
    info!("TEST PASS: test_random_read_iops");
}

#[test]
fn test_fsync_latency() {
    info!("TEST START: test_fsync_latency");
    let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    info!("INPUT: fsync_benchmark() with 100 iterations");
    let latency_ms = fsync_benchmark(temp_dir.path());

    assert!(latency_ms > 0.0);
    assert!(latency_ms < 100.0);  // Less than 100ms
    info!("VERIFY: fsync latency {} ms within reasonable range", latency_ms);
    info!("TEST PASS: test_fsync_latency");
}
```

## Goals

2. Create ADRs for key architectural decisions

5. Document extension points and plugin interfaces

## Deliverables

### 1. Classifier Architecture (docs/architecture/classifier.md)

```markdown
# 5-Tier Command Classifier

## Overview

The RCH classifier determines whether a command should be executed locally or remotely.
It uses a 5-tier system for fast rejection of non-compilation commands while accurately
identifying compilation workloads.

## Tier Descriptions

### Tier 0: Fast Negative Filter (SIMD)
- **Latency**: ~1µs
- **Purpose**: Instantly reject clearly non-compilation commands
- **Method**: SIMD keyword search for shell commands, utilities, file operations
- **Keywords**: `cd`, `ls`, `cat`, `echo`, `grep`, `awk`, `sed`, `rm`, `mv`, `cp`, `chmod`, `chown`, `mkdir`, `touch`, `find`, `sort`, `uniq`, `wc`, `head`, `tail`, `less`, `more`, `vi`, `vim`, `nano`, `git`, `ssh`, `scp`, `curl`, `wget`, `ping`, `nc`, `kill`, `ps`, `top`, `df`, `du`, `tar`, `gzip`, `zip`, `unzip`

Example matches (REJECT):
- `cd /path/to/dir` → Tier 0 reject (contains 'cd')
- `cat file.txt | grep foo` → Tier 0 reject (contains 'cat', 'grep')
- `git status` → Tier 0 reject (contains 'git')

### Tier 1: Positive Keyword Match
- **Latency**: ~5µs
- **Purpose**: Identify likely compilation commands
- **Method**: Check for build tool names and compilation flags
- **Keywords**: `cargo`, `rustc`, `gcc`, `g++`, `clang`, `clang++`, `make`, `cmake`, `ninja`, `meson`, `bazel`, `buck`, `scons`
- **Flags**: `-c`, `-o`, `-O`, `-g`, `-W`, `-std=`, `-march=`, `-mtune=`

Example matches (CANDIDATE):
- `cargo build` → Tier 1 match (contains 'cargo')
- `gcc -c foo.c -o foo.o` → Tier 1 match (contains 'gcc', '-c', '-o')

### Tier 2: Command Parser Analysis
- **Latency**: ~50µs
- **Purpose**: Parse command structure to identify build invocations
- **Method**: Shell parsing to extract base command and arguments
- **Handles**: Pipes, redirections, command substitution, environment variables

Example analysis:
- `RUSTFLAGS="-C target-cpu=native" cargo build --release`
  - Env: RUSTFLAGS
  - Base command: cargo
  - Subcommand: build
  - Flags: --release
  - Classification: COMPILATION_CANDIDATE

### Tier 3: Heuristic Scoring
- **Latency**: ~100µs
- **Purpose**: Score compilation likelihood for ambiguous commands
- **Factors**:
  - Source file extensions in arguments (.rs, .c, .cpp, .cc, .h, .hpp)
  - Presence of `-c` (compile only), `-o` (output), optimization flags
  - Working directory heuristics (contains Cargo.toml, Makefile, CMakeLists.txt)
  - Historical patterns (this command compiled before)

Scoring example:
```
Command: `rustc lib.rs -o lib`
- rustc binary: +50 points
- .rs extension: +20 points
- -o flag: +10 points
Total: 80 points (threshold: 50)
Decision: COMPILATION
```

### Tier 4: Machine Learning Model (Optional)
- **Latency**: ~500µs
- **Purpose**: Handle edge cases with learned patterns
- **Model**: Small decision tree or random forest
- **Features**: Command tokens, file extensions, directory context, time of day
- **Training**: From actual compilation logs

## Negative Pattern Handling

Commands that look like compilation but should NOT be remoted:

| Pattern | Reason | Example |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `cargo test` | Tests should run locally | May need local fixtures |
| `cargo run` | Execution, not compilation | Output goes to local terminal |
| `make install` | System modification | Needs local permissions |
| `cargo doc` | Documentation | Generates local files |
| `--help` | Help text | Local information |
| `--version` | Version info | Local binary version |

## Edge Cases

### Pipes and Subshells
```bash
# Should NOT remote (output piped)
cargo build 2>&1 | tee build.log

# Should remote (input from file, compilation command)
cargo build < config.txt
```

### Command Substitution
```bash
# Should NOT remote (complex shell interaction)
$(cargo build --message-format=json | jq ...)

# Should remote (simple build)
cargo build --features=$(cat features.txt)
```

### Multiple Commands
```bash
# First command only matters if &&
cargo build && ./target/debug/myapp  # Remote the build, not the run

# Both analyzed if ;
cargo build; cargo test  # Build: remote, Test: local
```

## Performance Budget

| Tier | Target Latency | Max Memory |
|------|----------------|------------|
| 0 | 1µs | 0 |
| 1 | 5µs | 0 |
| 2 | 50µs | 1KB |
| 3 | 100µs | 10KB |
| 4 | 500µs | 1MB |
| Total (95th percentile) | < 200µs | < 100KB |

## Benchmarks

Run classification benchmarks:
```bash
cargo bench --bench classifier
```

Expected results on modern hardware (M1/Ryzen 5000):
- Simple reject (Tier 0): 200ns
- Simple accept (Tier 1): 1µs
- Complex parse (Tier 2): 10µs
- Full heuristic (Tier 3): 50µs
```

**ADR-001: Unix Socket for IPC (docs/adr/001-unix-socket-ipc.md)**
```markdown
# ADR-001: Unix Socket for Daemon IPC

## Status
Accepted

## Context
The RCH CLI needs to communicate with the daemon for build classification and execution.
Options considered:
1. Unix domain socket
2. TCP socket
3. Shared memory
4. Named pipes

## Decision
Use Unix domain sockets for IPC.

## Consequences
### Positive
- Zero network overhead
- Built-in permission model (file permissions)
- Reliable delivery guarantees
- Efficient for small messages

### Negative
- Not portable to Windows (though we can use named pipes there)
- File system state to manage (socket file)

## Alternatives Considered
- TCP: Added network stack overhead, port management
- Shared memory: Complex synchronization, harder debugging
- Named pipes: Less flexible, no multiplexing
```

```markdown
# Debugging Slow Builds

## Symptoms
- Build takes longer than expected
- `rch status` shows high latency to workers
- Builds waiting in queue

## Diagnostic Steps

### 1. Check Worker Health
```bash
rch status --workers
```
Look for:
- Workers marked "degraded" or "unavailable"
- High latency values (>100ms)
- Low available slots

### 2. Check Circuit Breaker State
```bash
rch status --circuits
```
If circuits are open:
- Worker is experiencing failures
- Wait for half-open state or investigate worker

### 3. Check Transfer Performance
```bash
RCH_LOG_LEVEL=debug rch build 2>&1 | grep -i transfer
```
Look for:
- Transfer times >5s for small projects
- Compression ratios <2x (might need different level)

### 4. Check Classification
```bash
rch classify "your command here"
```
Verify the command is being classified correctly.

## Common Solutions

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| All circuits open | Check network, restart workers |
| High transfer time | Check bandwidth, adjust compression |
| Wrong classification | Report bug, use --local flag |
| Queue backup | Add workers or reduce parallel builds |
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Extract and test code examples from documentation

    log "Testing classifier examples..."

    # Extract examples from classifier.md
    grep -A1 "Example matches" "$DOCS_DIR/architecture/classifier.md" | \
        grep -E "^\`.*\`" | while read -r example; do
            CMD=$(echo "$example" | sed 's/`//g' | cut -d'→' -f1 | xargs)
            EXPECTED=$(echo "$example" | grep -oE "(REJECT|CANDIDATE|COMPILATION)")

            log "  Testing: $CMD → expected $EXPECTED"

            # Run actual classifier
            RESULT=$(cargo run --quiet -- classify "$CMD" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")
            if ! echo "$RESULT" | grep -qi "$EXPECTED"; then
                log "  MISMATCH: got $RESULT"
            fi
        done
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
DOCS_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../docs"
RCH="${RCH:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../target/release/rch}"
TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
LOG_FILE="$TEST_DIR/e2e_docs.log"

log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; exit 1; }

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "=== RCH Documentation E2E Test ==="
log "Docs dir: $DOCS_DIR"

# Test 1: All required documentation files exist
test_docs_exist() {
    log "Test 1: Required documentation files exist"

    REQUIRED_FILES=(
        "architecture/classifier.md"
        "adr/001-unix-socket-ipc.md"
        "diagrams/components.md"
        "runbooks/debugging-slow-builds.md"
    )

    for file in "${REQUIRED_FILES[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "$DOCS_DIR/$file" ]]; then
            log "  Found: $file"
        else
            fail "Missing: $file"
        fi
    done

    pass "Documentation files exist"
}

# Test 2: Classifier examples are accurate
test_classifier_accuracy() {
    log "Test 2: Classifier examples match implementation"

    # Test Tier 0 rejects
    TIER0_REJECTS=("cd /tmp" "ls -la" "cat file.txt" "git status" "grep foo bar")
    for cmd in "${TIER0_REJECTS[@]}"; do
        RESULT=$("$RCH" classify "$cmd" 2>&1 || echo "LOCAL")
        log "  '$cmd' → $RESULT"
        if ! echo "$RESULT" | grep -qiE "local|reject|tier.0"; then
            log "    Warning: expected reject/local"
        fi
    done

    # Test Tier 1 candidates
    TIER1_CANDIDATES=("cargo build" "rustc lib.rs" "gcc main.c" "make all")
    for cmd in "${TIER1_CANDIDATES[@]}"; do
        RESULT=$("$RCH" classify "$cmd" 2>&1 || echo "UNKNOWN")
        log "  '$cmd' → $RESULT"
        if ! echo "$RESULT" | grep -qiE "remote|candidate|tier.1|compilation"; then
            log "    Warning: expected remote/candidate"
        fi
    done

    pass "Classifier accuracy"
}

# Test 3: ADR format is valid
test_adr_format() {
    log "Test 3: ADR format validation"

    for adr in "$DOCS_DIR"/adr/*.md; do
        NAME=$(basename "$adr")
        log "  Checking $NAME..."

        # Must have Status section
        if ! grep -q "^## Status" "$adr"; then
            fail "$NAME missing Status section"
        fi

        # Must have Decision section
        if ! grep -q "^## Decision" "$adr"; then
            fail "$NAME missing Decision section"
        fi

        # Must have Context section
        if ! grep -q "^## Context" "$adr"; then
            fail "$NAME missing Context section"
        fi

        log "    Format OK"
    done

    pass "ADR format"
}

# Test 4: Runbook commands are valid
test_runbook_commands() {
    log "Test 4: Runbook command validation"

    for runbook in "$DOCS_DIR"/runbooks/*.md; do
        NAME=$(basename "$runbook")
        log "  Checking $NAME..."

        # Extract command examples
        grep -E "^rch " "$runbook" 2>/dev/null | while read -r cmd; do
            # Verify command structure (subcommand exists)
            SUBCMD=$(echo "$cmd" | awk '{print $2}')
            if "$RCH" "$SUBCMD" --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                log "    '$cmd' → valid subcommand"
            else
                log "    '$cmd' → Note: subcommand '$SUBCMD' may not exist yet"
            fi
        done
    done

    pass "Runbook commands"
}

# Test 5: Links are not broken
test_internal_links() {
    log "Test 5: Internal link validation"

    BROKEN=0
    find "$DOCS_DIR" -name "*.md" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
        # Find markdown links
        grep -oE '\[.+\]\([^)]+\)' "$file" 2>/dev/null | while read -r link; do
            TARGET=$(echo "$link" | grep -oE '\([^)]+\)' | tr -d '()')

            # Skip external links
            if [[ "$TARGET" =~ ^http ]]; then
                continue
            fi

            # Resolve relative path
            DIR=$(dirname "$file")
            FULL_PATH="$DIR/$TARGET"

            if [[ ! -f "$FULL_PATH" ]] && [[ ! -d "$FULL_PATH" ]]; then
                log "  Broken link in $(basename "$file"): $TARGET"
                BROKEN=$((BROKEN + 1))
            fi
        done
    done

    if [[ $BROKEN -gt 0 ]]; then
        log "  Found $BROKEN broken links"
    fi
    pass "Internal links"
}

# Test 6: Diagrams render properly (basic check)
test_diagrams() {
    log "Test 6: Diagram validation"

    for diagram in "$DOCS_DIR"/diagrams/*.md; do
        NAME=$(basename "$diagram")
        log "  Checking $NAME..."

        # Check for proper box drawing characters
        if grep -q "┌" "$diagram" && grep -q "└" "$diagram"; then
            log "    Box characters present"
        else
            log "    Note: May use different diagram format"
        fi

        # Check diagram isn't empty
        LINES=$(wc -l < "$diagram")
        if [[ $LINES -lt 10 ]]; then
            log "    Warning: diagram seems short ($LINES lines)"
        fi
    done

    pass "Diagrams"
}

test_docs_exist
test_classifier_accuracy
test_adr_format
test_runbook_commands
test_internal_links
test_diagrams

log "=== All Documentation E2E tests passed ==="
log "Full log at: $LOG_FILE"

Implement SSH-based remote compilation verification that tests the full RCH pipeline by building code on a remote worker and verifying the output.

## Background and Justification
The ultimate self-test must verify the complete pipeline:
1. rsync source files to worker
2. Execute cargo build remotely
3. rsync artifacts back
4. Verify binary correctness via hash comparison

This tests all components working together.

## Implementation Details

### Verification Flow
```rust
pub struct RemoteCompilationTest {
    worker: WorkerConfig,
    test_project: PathBuf,
    timeout: Duration,
}

pub struct VerificationResult {
    pub success: bool,
    pub local_hash: BinaryHashResult,
    pub remote_hash: BinaryHashResult,
    pub rsync_up_ms: u64,
    pub compilation_ms: u64,
    pub rsync_down_ms: u64,
    pub total_ms: u64,
    pub error: Option<String>,
}

impl RemoteCompilationTest {
    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<VerificationResult> {
        let start = Instant::now();
        
        // 1. Apply test change to make binary unique
        let change = TestCodeChange::for_main_rs(&self.test_project)?;
        let _guard = TestChangeGuard::new(change)?;
        
        // 2. Build locally first
        info!("Building locally for reference hash");
        let local_build_start = Instant::now();
        self.build_local().await?;
        let local_hash = compute_binary_hash(&self.local_binary_path())?;
        info!("Local build complete: hash={}", local_hash.code_hash);
        
        // 3. rsync up to worker
        info!("Syncing source to worker {}", self.worker.id);
        let rsync_up_start = Instant::now();
        self.rsync_to_worker().await?;
        let rsync_up_ms = rsync_up_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;
        
        // 4. Build on worker
        info!("Building on remote worker");
        let compile_start = Instant::now();
        self.build_remote().await?;
        let compilation_ms = compile_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;
        
        // 5. rsync back
        info!("Syncing artifacts from worker");
        let rsync_down_start = Instant::now();
        self.rsync_from_worker().await?;
        let rsync_down_ms = rsync_down_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;
        
        // 6. Compute remote binary hash
        let remote_hash = compute_binary_hash(&self.remote_binary_path())?;
        info!("Remote build complete: hash={}", remote_hash.code_hash);
        
        // 7. Compare
        let success = binaries_equivalent(&local_hash, &remote_hash);
        
        Ok(VerificationResult {
            success,
            local_hash,
            remote_hash,
            rsync_up_ms,
            compilation_ms,
            rsync_down_ms,
            total_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
            error: if success { None } else { 
                Some("Binary hashes do not match".into()) 
            },
        })
    }
    
    async fn build_local(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let status = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["build", "--release"])
            .current_dir(&self.test_project)
            .env("CARGO_INCREMENTAL", "0")
            .status()
            .await?;
        
        if !status.success() {
            anyhow::bail!("Local build failed");
        }
        Ok(())
    }
    
    async fn rsync_to_worker(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let remote_path = format!("{}:{}", 
            self.worker.ssh_host, 
            self.worker.build_dir.join("self_test")
        );
        
        let status = Command::new("rsync")
            .args([
                "-az", "--delete",
                "--exclude", "target/",
                &self.test_project.to_string_lossy(),
                &remote_path,
            ])
            .status()
            .await?;
        
        if !status.success() {
            anyhow::bail!("rsync to worker failed");
        }
        Ok(())
    }
    
    async fn build_remote(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let remote_project = self.worker.build_dir
            .join("self_test")
            .join(self.test_project.file_name().unwrap());
        
        let status = Command::new("ssh")
            .args([
                &self.worker.ssh_host,
                &format!(
                    "cd {} && cargo build --release",
                    remote_project.display()
                ),
            ])
            .status()
            .await?;
        
        if !status.success() {
            anyhow::bail!("Remote build failed");
        }
        Ok(())
    }
    
    async fn rsync_from_worker(&self) -> Result<()> {
        // Sync back just the target/release directory
        let remote_target = format!("{}:{}/target/release/",
            self.worker.ssh_host,
            self.worker.build_dir.join("self_test").join(
                self.test_project.file_name().unwrap()
            ).display()
        );
        
        let local_target = self.test_project.join("target/release_remote/");
        fs::create_dir_all(&local_target)?;
        
        let status = Command::new("rsync")
            .args(["-az", &remote_target, &local_target.to_string_lossy()])
            .status()
            .await?;
        
        if !status.success() {
            anyhow::bail!("rsync from worker failed");
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

│   ├── generics.rs      # Generic code (monomorphization stress)
│   ├── traits.rs        # Trait implementations

```

### Benchmark Implementation
```rust
use std::process::Command;
use std::time::Instant;
use tempfile::TempDir;

pub struct CompilationBenchmarkResult {
    pub score: f64,
    pub debug_build_ms: u64,
    pub release_build_ms: u64,
    pub incremental_build_ms: u64,
    pub rustc_version: String,
}

/// Extract bundled benchmark project to temp directory
fn setup_benchmark_project(temp_dir: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf> {
    // Benchmark project is embedded as include_bytes! or extracted from resources
    let project_archive = include_bytes!("../resources/rch_benchmark.tar.gz");
    
    // Extract to temp directory
    let tar_gz = flate2::read::GzDecoder::new(&project_archive[..]);
    let mut archive = tar::Archive::new(tar_gz);
    archive.unpack(temp_dir)?;
    
    Ok(temp_dir.join("rch_benchmark_project"))
}

/// Run cargo build and measure time
fn timed_cargo_build(project_dir: &Path, release: bool) -> Result<u64> {
    // Clean first
    Command::new("cargo")
        .arg("clean")
        .current_dir(project_dir)
        .output()?;
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    let status = Command::new("cargo")
        .arg("build")
        .args(if release { vec!["--release"] } else { vec![] })
        .current_dir(project_dir)
        .env("CARGO_INCREMENTAL", "0")
        .output()?;
    
    if !status.status.success() {
        anyhow::bail!("Cargo build failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&status.stderr));
    }
    
    Ok(start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64)
}

/// Measure incremental rebuild time
fn timed_incremental_build(project_dir: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
    // First do a full build
    Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "--release"])
        .current_dir(project_dir)
        .env("CARGO_INCREMENTAL", "1")
        .output()?;
    
    // Touch a source file to trigger incremental rebuild
    let main_rs = project_dir.join("src/main.rs");
    let content = fs::read_to_string(&main_rs)?;
    fs::write(&main_rs, format!("{}\n// touched", content))?;
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "--release"])
        .current_dir(project_dir)
        .env("CARGO_INCREMENTAL", "1")
        .output()?;
    
    Ok(start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64)
}

pub fn run_compilation_benchmark() -> Result<CompilationBenchmarkResult> {
    let temp_dir = TempDir::new()?;
    let project_dir = setup_benchmark_project(temp_dir.path())?;
    
    // Get rustc version
    let version_output = Command::new("rustc").arg("--version").output()?;
    let rustc_version = String::from_utf8_lossy(&version_output.stdout).trim().to_string();
    
    // Run benchmarks
    let debug_ms = timed_cargo_build(&project_dir, false)?;
    let release_ms = timed_cargo_build(&project_dir, true)?;
    let incremental_ms = timed_incremental_build(&project_dir)?;
    
    // Score based on release build time (lower = better)
    // Reference: 10 seconds on M1 MacBook Pro = 1000 score
    let reference_ms = 10_000.0;
    let score = (reference_ms / release_ms as f64) * 1000.0;
    
    Ok(CompilationBenchmarkResult {
        score,
        debug_build_ms: debug_ms,
        release_build_ms: release_ms,
        incremental_build_ms: incremental_ms,
        rustc_version,
    })
}

    let version_output = Command::new("rustc").arg("--version").output()?;
    let rustc_version = String::from_utf8_lossy(&version_output.stdout).trim().to_string();
    
    // Run benchmarks
    let debug_ms = timed_cargo_build(&project_dir, false)?;
    let release_ms = timed_cargo_build(&project_dir, true)?;
    let incremental_ms = timed_incremental_build(&project_dir)?;
    
    // Score based on release build time (lower = better)
    // Reference: 10 seconds on M1 MacBook Pro = 1000 score
    let reference_ms = 10_000.0;
    let score = (reference_ms / release_ms as f64) * 1000.0;
    
    Ok(CompilationBenchmarkResult {
        score,
        debug_build_ms: debug_ms,
        release_build_ms: release_ms,
        incremental_build_ms: incremental_ms,
        rustc_version,
    })
}

## Implementation

### CLI Module Structure
```rust
// rch/src/commands/speedscore.rs

use clap::{Args, Subcommand};

#[derive(Args)]
pub struct SpeedScoreArgs {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    pub command: Option<SpeedScoreCommand>,
    
    /// Worker ID to show SpeedScore for
    pub worker_id: Option<String>,
    
    /// Show historical scores
    #[arg(long)]
    pub history: bool,
    
    /// Number of days of history to show
    #[arg(long, default_value = "30")]
    pub days: u32,
    
    /// Output format (table, json, csv)
    #[arg(long, default_value = "table")]
    pub format: OutputFormat,
}

#[derive(Subcommand)]
pub enum SpeedScoreCommand {
    /// List all workers with SpeedScores
    List(SpeedScoreListArgs),
    /// Show detailed SpeedScore for a worker
    Show(SpeedScoreShowArgs),
    /// Compare multiple workers
    Compare(SpeedScoreCompareArgs),
}

pub async fn handle_speedscore(args: SpeedScoreArgs, client: &RchdClient) -> Result<()> {
    match args.command {
        Some(SpeedScoreCommand::List(list_args)) => {
            let scores = client.get_all_speedscores().await?;
            render_speedscore_list(&scores, &list_args)?;
        }
        Some(SpeedScoreCommand::Show(show_args)) => {
            let score = client.get_speedscore(&show_args.worker_id).await?;
            render_speedscore_detail(&score)?;
        }
        Some(SpeedScoreCommand::Compare(compare_args)) => {
            let scores = client.get_speedscores(&compare_args.worker_ids).await?;
            render_speedscore_comparison(&scores)?;
        }
        None if args.worker_id.is_some() => {
            let worker_id = args.worker_id.unwrap();
            if args.history {
                let history = client.get_speedscore_history(&worker_id, args.days).await?;
                render_speedscore_history(&history)?;
            } else {
                let score = client.get_speedscore(&worker_id).await?;
                render_speedscore_detail(&score)?;
            }
        }
        None => {
            // Default: list all workers with scores
            let scores = client.get_all_speedscores().await?;
            render_speedscore_list(&scores, &SpeedScoreListArgs::default())?;
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Progress Bar for Benchmarks
```rust
// rch/src/commands/benchmark.rs

use indicatif::{MultiProgress, ProgressBar, ProgressStyle};

pub async fn run_benchmark_with_progress(
    client: &RchdClient,
    worker_id: &str,
    force: bool,
) -> Result<SpeedScore> {
    let mp = MultiProgress::new();
    let phases = ["CPU", "Memory", "Disk", "Network", "Compilation"];
    
    let bars: Vec<ProgressBar> = phases.iter().map(|name| {
        let pb = mp.add(ProgressBar::new(100));
        pb.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_bar()
            .template("{prefix:12} {bar:40.cyan/blue} {pos:>3}% {msg}")
            .unwrap());
        pb.set_prefix(format!("[{}/5] {}", phases.iter().position(|&n| n == *name).unwrap() + 1, name));
        pb
    }).collect();
    
    // Subscribe to progress updates via WebSocket
    let mut rx = client.subscribe_benchmark_progress(worker_id).await?;
    
    // Trigger benchmark
    client.trigger_benchmark(worker_id, force).await?;
    
    while let Some(update) = rx.recv().await {
        match update {
            BenchmarkProgress::PhaseProgress { phase, progress_pct } => {
                let idx = phases.iter().position(|&p| p == phase).unwrap();
                bars[idx].set_position(progress_pct as u64);
            }
            BenchmarkProgress::PhaseComplete { phase, score } => {
                let idx = phases.iter().position(|&p| p == phase).unwrap();
                bars[idx].finish_with_message(format!("Score: {}", score));
            }
            BenchmarkProgress::Complete { speedscore } => {
                mp.clear()?;
                return Ok(speedscore);
            }
            BenchmarkProgress::Failed { error, phase } => {
                mp.clear()?;
                return Err(anyhow!("Benchmark failed during {}: {}", phase, error));
            }
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow!("Benchmark stream ended unexpectedly"))
}
